lines.text: Switching to channel two. [pause] Let's talk this over privately, gentlemen.
[flatly] We'll talk right here. Whole world's listening anyway.
[deliberate] All right. On the record, then. Who are you, and who sent you up here.
[flatly] We're the backup crew. NASA never processed us out. We're still on the roster, son. This is our mission.
Where did you even COME from?
[tired] This here's our camp. Pack the fuck off.
[deliberate] I just want the one piece of paper that takes them off the Moon. Somebody go find me that.

[deliberate] It's been signed off every quarter since 1972, by career staff, under four statutes. He's not a trespasser. As far as the United States government is concerned, that man is federal personnel standing on his duty station.
(reading) [deliberate] Dawes, E. Astronaut, active. And the whole pay column's zeros, every quarter, back fifty years.
(then) [deliberate] Then send somebody up there and remove them.
[deliberate] Who? The only Americans on the Moon are the ones you want removed.
[deliberate] And sir, the President offered him the wings on a recorded line Tuesday. We can't call him a fraud without calling the President one.
(into radio) [deliberate] That's the colors, son. You'll stop right there.
(into radio) Harmony copies. [pause] Control, Harmony requests instructions.
